notes: Overall the session felt pretty good. I do think that my shoulders and biceps were still a little tired from Saturday. We did get a little extra shoulder and bicep work in during the HIIT class but I was able to push through and get most of the reps here today.

exercises.note: Got 12 reps on both sets of the hanging knee raise. I probably could have gotten another set in easily.

notes: Overall this was a good workout. I felt pretty strong throughout the whole thing. I definitely feel worked right now at the end but not too burnt. I was able to manage all the weights, all the reps clean, and I feel good.

exercises.note: The tricep pushdown was tough but I got all of the reps. I think it was tough because of the skull crushers that I did earlier in the week, which was the first time I did that and they gave me a little bit extra DOMS. I got all of the curls. I did 12 reps on the last set of the curls.

2 rounds

notes:
I think this was a pretty good session. I had a rough weekend:
- Friday, I dealt with some personal stress.
- Saturday, I drank way more than I normally do, alcohol.
- Sunday was like a hangover day.


For having that situation I feel like the workout was actually really good today. I think if I didn't have a hangover yesterday, I would have been able to get a little even bit more in.

notes:
Honestly I didn't get the best sleep last night. I had a high-fat meal, which I haven't had in a while, at night and my stomach was kind of upset. I contemplated not coming to the gym this morning because I felt tired and maybe I should have just listened to my body instead of pushing it. I did do a big hike on Saturday as well after my upper body workout. I probably should have waited until Tuesday and got a full two days of rest, like I normally do on the weekend or at some point during the week, and I just didn't give myself that opportunity. Hopefully this upper back pull is minor and it will go away in a week or so. 

Also worth noting: I got to the gym later than I normally do and the treadmills were taken. Instead of doing my normal 8-minute walk before starting, I did a series of stretching, like hip extension stretches and some downward dog and some jumping jacks. I felt like I was loose enough to go. The leg press and hip thrust felt good so I feel like I was good to go. If I did my 8-minute walk instead in the beginning, I'm wondering if I would have been more warm somehow or if the 65 lb dumbbell on the deadlift today was just a little too much load for me.

On my first set around the tenth rep, I felt something in my upper back between my shoulder blades pull a bit so I stopped. Doing the deadlifts and went straight to the curls and yeah I'm on curls right now. My back's feeling tight.

notes: I bounced around a little bit with the exercises today. Obviously I got the lunges in actually before the leg curls so maybe I would have actually gotten 12 on the last set if I didn't do the lunges right before. Just a note, overall my back felt fine throughout the whole entire exercise and it's still definitely tight but I can definitely get some good workouts in while I am being cautious of that spot.
